zone_6b_notes: The defining shade perennial for Zone 6b — hostas thrive under trees and in the north-facing shaded areas of Farmington gardens. Reliable, long-lived, and low maintenance once established. Missouri's humid summers favor hostas over drier climates. Slug damage is the primary problem — use iron phosphate bait (Sluggo) in spring and after heavy rains. Deer eat hostas readily — fence or use deterrents if deer pressure is high. Divide every 5–6 years in spring when eyes first emerge. Blue-leafed varieties (Halcyon, Elegans) hold color better with more shade; gold varieties (Sum and Substance) tolerate more sun.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant or divide in spring; nursery transplants in spring or fall

water: consistent — 1 inch/week; drought causes leaf scorch
